lede: A look at September 1 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Kirani James and Leonel Suárez, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Titanic
events.desc: The wreck of the Titanic is discovered by an American-French expedition led by Robert Ballard and Jean-Louis Michel.

events.title: Cold War
events.desc: Cold War: Korean Air Lines Flight 007 is shot down by a Soviet jet fighter after the commercial aircraft strayed into Soviet airspace, killing all 269 on board, including Congressman Lawrence McDonald.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Launch of Operation Ratweek, complicating German retreat.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Confederate forces under General Stonewall Jackson inflict heavy casualties in the Battle of Chantilly during the Union withdrawal from the Northern Virginia campaign.

events.title: Carrington Event
events.desc: Carrington Event started - the most intense geomagnetic storm in recorded history.

events.title: 3 Juno
events.desc: 3 Juno, one of the largest asteroids in the Main Belt, is discovered by the German astronomer Karl Ludwig Harding.

events.title: Catherine the Great
events.desc: Catherine the Great, Empress of Russia, endorses Ivan Betskoy's plans for a Foundling Home in Moscow.

events.title: Louis XV
events.desc: At the age of five, Louis XV becomes king of France in succession to his great-grandfather, King Louis XIV.

events.title: Guru Granth Sahib
events.desc: Adi Granth, now known as Guru Granth Sahib, the holy scripture of Sikhs, is first installed at Harmandir Sahib.

events.title: Lund Cathedral
events.desc: The main altar of Lund Cathedral, at the time the seat of the archiepiscopal see of all the Nordic countries, is consecrated.

births.name: Roh Moo-hyun
births.line: South Korean soldier and politician, 9th President of South Korea

deaths.text: Otl Aicher, German graphic designer and typographer
Henry XIV, Duke of Bavaria, 13th-century Bavarian nobleman

observances.text: Christian feast day: David Pendleton Oakerhater (Anglican Communion)
